summary: Frontend UI testing for Purchase & Sales Return modules (Phase 1 MVP) completed successfully. All three return pages load correctly without errors. Filter dropdowns work properly with 'All' as default value (no SelectItem empty value errors). Navigation links in Finance submenu are present and functional.

minor.issue: Returns 405 Method Not Allowed when fetching invoices for Purchase Returns modal
Returns 404 Not Found when fetching invoices for Sales Returns modal

integration_issues.issue: Invoice dropdown may be empty due to 405 error from execution-ledger API
Invoice dropdown may be empty due to 404 error from /api/invoices

passed_tests: Purchase Returns page (/finance/purchase-returns) loads correctly
Purchase Returns - Page title and subtitle display correctly
Purchase Returns - Refund Status filter dropdown works without errors
Purchase Returns - Item Disposition filter dropdown works without errors
Purchase Returns - Date filters (From Date, To Date) present
Purchase Returns - Clear button works
Purchase Returns - Empty state 'No purchase returns found' displays correctly
Sales Returns page (/finance/sales-returns) loads correctly
Sales Returns - Page title and subtitle display correctly
Sales Returns - Refund Status filter dropdown works (All, Pending, Partial, Completed, Adjusted, No Refund)
Sales Returns - Item Disposition filter dropdown works (7 options including Reused in Other Project)
Sales Returns - Date filters present
Sales Returns - Empty state 'No sales returns found' displays correctly
Returned Items Register page (/finance/returned-items-register) loads correctly
Returned Items Register - Page title and subtitle display correctly
Returned Items Register - Summary cards display (Total Returns, With Company, Returned to Vendor, Scrapped/Rejected, Pending Refunds)
Returned Items Register - Return Type filter dropdown works (All, Purchase, Sales)
Returned Items Register - Item Disposition filter dropdown works
Returned Items Register - Refund Status filter dropdown works
Returned Items Register - Navigation buttons (Purchase Returns, Sales Returns) present and functional
Returned Items Register - data-testid attributes present for testing
Returned Items Register - Empty state with helpful message displays correctly
Finance submenu - Purchase Returns link present
Finance submenu - Sales Returns link present
Finance submenu - Returns Register link present
All filter dropdowns use 'All' value instead of empty string (no SelectItem errors)
action_items: Note: 'New Return' buttons on Purchase Returns and Sales Returns pages are permission-gated (finance.liabilities.create and finance.invoices.create). Founder role may not have these permissions - this is expected behavior.
Minor: The execution-ledger API returns 405 and /api/invoices returns 404 - these may affect the create return modals but don't break the main page functionality.
critical_code_review_comments: PurchaseReturns.jsx: Filter dropdowns correctly use 'all' value with conversion to empty string for API calls (lines 280, 295)
SalesReturns.jsx: Filter dropdowns correctly use 'all' value with conversion to empty string for API calls (lines 288, 304)
ReturnedItemsRegister.jsx: Filter dropdowns correctly use 'all' value with conversion to empty string for API calls (lines 233, 246, 260)
Sidebar.jsx: Finance submenu correctly includes Purchase Returns, Sales Returns, and Returns Register links (lines 73-75)
App.js: Routes correctly defined for all three return pages (lines 180-182)
All pages have proper data-testid attributes for testing
seed_data_creation: None - tested with empty data state
context_for_next_testing_agent: Purchase & Sales Return module frontend UI is complete and working. All three pages (Purchase Returns, Sales Returns, Returned Items Register) load correctly with proper filter dropdowns. The 'New Return' buttons are permission-gated and may not be visible for all roles. Backend APIs for returns are working (tested by main agent via curl). Minor issues: execution-ledger API returns 405 and /api/invoices returns 404 which may affect invoice selection in create modals.
